title: Validation of Protocol to Assess Calcium Binding to S. mutans
author: Livia M A Tenuta
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kuqcwvw

steps.title: Reactivate Streptococcus mutans Ingbritt 1600 cells...
steps.instruction: Reactivate Streptococcus mutans Ingbritt 1600 cells by inoculating 20 uL of a glycerol-frozen culture in 10 mL of Brain-Heart Infusion (BHI) broth. Allow the BHI broth to grow at 37°C, 10% CO 2 , for 18 h. Streak the BHI culture on blood agar to obtain isolated colonies. Incubate at 37°C, 10% CO 2 , for 18 h. Observe the purity of the culture by colony morphology. Store the plate at 4°C for up to one month. Tranfer 6-7 isolated colonies from the blood agar to 100 mL of thioglycolate-based medium (2.4% of thioglycolate, 1.2% of meat extract, 1.5% of calcium carbonate and 0.5% of glucose). Allow the thioglycolate medium to grow for 18h. Check the purity of the culture by examining a 10 uL aliquot of the fresh culture at a microscope and also by Gram staining. Store the thioglycolate medium at 4°C for up to 7 days. Transfer 8 mL of the thioglycolate medium to 500 mL of a Tryptone-yeast extract (TYB) broth (2.5% of tryptone, 1.5% of yeast extract, 0.43% of potassium hydrogen phosphate and 0.1% of magnesium sulfate heptahydrate), pH 7.0 supplemented with 0.25% glucose. Allow the TYB broth to grow at 37°C, 10% CO 2 , for 18 h.

steps.title: After TYB growth for 18 h,...
steps.instruction: After TYB growth for 18 h, centrifuge all content of the culture at 10,000 x g, 4°C, for 10 min. Discard the supernatant broth and resuspend the whole bacterial pellets in 100 mL of 0.05 M PIPES buffer, pH 7.0, at 4 °C (use 2 x 50-mL tubes). Repeat all the steps below for each 50 mL tube. Vortex mix for 30 s and sonicate the suspension at 7 W for 1 min. Centrifuge at 10,000 x g, 4°C for 10 min. Discard the supernatant and resuspend in 50 mL of 0.01 M EDTA solution, 4°C (in order to chelate calcium). Vortex mix for 30 s and sonicate the suspension at 7 W for 1 min. Centrifuge at 10,000 x g, 4°C for 10 min. Discard the supernatant and resuspend in 50 mL of 0.05 M PIPES buffer, pH 7.0, at 4°C. Vortex mix for 30 s and sonicate the suspension at 7 W for 1 min. Centrifuge at 10,000 g, 4°C for 10 min and discard the supernatant. Resuspend in about 10 mL of 0.05 M PIPES buffer, pH 7.0, at 4 °C. Vortex mix for 30 s and transfer aliquots of about 1.4 mL and 0.9 mL to pre-weighted 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tubes to harvest bacterial pellets of aproximately 120 mg and 80 mg, respectively. Centrifuge the microcentrifuge tubes at 21,000 x g, 4 °C for 5 min to expel all the liquid from the bacterial pellet. Carefully discard the supernatant under microscope using a vaccum aspiration system and glass micropipettes. Repeat steps 14 and 15. Weigh the microcentrifuge tubes. In order to obtain about 120 mg or 80 mg of bacterial pellets, the volume of PIPES buffer mentioned in step 12 and the volume to be aliquoted in microcentrifuge tubes mentioned in step 13 should be previously determined in pilot studies, according to the bacterial yield.

steps.title: Obtain the weight of the S
steps.instruction: Obtain the weight of the S. mutans pellet in each microcentrifuge tube. To each tube, add 1 or 10 mM Ca (from CaCl 2 ) containing 0.05 M PIPES buffer, pH 7.0, at 37°C as the treatment solution. In the 1 mM Ca treatments, use 15 µL/mg bacteria; in the 10 mM Ca treatments, use 7.5 µL/mg bacteria. Vortex mix for 30 s and maintain the tubes at 37°C for 60 min in a dry block to allow complete calcium binding to S. mutans. Centrifuge the microcentrifuge tubes at 21,000 x g, 4°C for 5 min. Collect the supernatant to a new tube to determine calcium concentration. Repeat step 4. Carefully discard remnants of the supernatant under microscope using a vaccum aspiration system and glass micropipettes. Determine calcium bound to the bacterial pellets after acid extraction. The final bacterial pellet obtained may be stored at -20ºC until the acid extraction of bound calcium. The supernatant may also be stored at -20ºC until determination of calcium concentration.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 M HCl to each bacterial pellet at a proportion of 0.1 mL/10 mg. Vortex mix until the pellet is completelly suspended in the acid. Maintain under agitation in a rotating mixer for 3 h at room temperature; at frequent intervals (approximately 30 min), vortex mix the samples. Centrifuge the microcentrifuge tubes at 10,000 x g, for 3 min, at room temperature. Collect the supernatant to measure calcium in the acid extract.

steps.title: in 96-well microplate
steps.instruction: in 96-well microplate. The standard curve can be prepared as described in the table 3 below. Samples can be prepared as described in table 4 below. Read the absorbance of the mixtures in a microplate reader at 650 nm. Calculate calcium concentration using linear regression. Table
